new_classes.label_en: Vinegar, fermented vinegar and substitutes for vinegar obtained from acetic acid (heading 2209)
new_classes.label_de: Speiseessig, Gärungsessig und Essigersatz aus Essigsäure (Position 2209)
new_classes.definition_en: Vinegar, fermented vinegar and substitutes for vinegar obtained from acetic acid, as classified under CN heading 2209.

Vinegar obtained by acetous fermentation of wine (from fresh grapes), classified under CN heading 2209. Includes both wine vinegar in containers ≤ 2 L (subheading 2209 00 11) and in containers > 2 L (subheading 2209 00 19).

new_classes.label_en: Wine acetic fermentation process (acetous fermentation of wine to produce wine vinegar)
new_classes.label_de: Essigsäuregärungsprozess aus Wein (acetische Fermentation von Wein zur Herstellung von Weinessig)
new_classes.definition_en: The acetous (acetic acid) fermentation process applied to wine (product of fresh grapes) to produce wine vinegar, distinguishing wine vinegar from other vinegars under heading 2209.

new_classes.label_de: Weinessig in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von höchstens 2 Litern (Unterposition 2209 00 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine vinegar presented in containers with a capacity of 2 litres or less, classified under CN subheading 2209 00 11 in accordance with additional note 11 to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation:
The main distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) the product is wine vinegar (i.e. vinegar derived from wine via acetous fermentation), modelled as a subclass of the new Vinegar2209 class with a production process restriction; (2) the container size ≤ 2 L criterion discriminating subheading 2209 00 11 from 2209 00 19, modelled via BFO has_part some SmallContainer. 

Not fully captured: (a) The reference to "additional note 11 to this chapter" — this is an administrative cross-reference whose content is not provided in the legal notes supplied; the specific conditions of that additional note (e.g. minimum acidity thresholds) cannot be modelled without the note's text, which reduces coverage. (b) The HS Explanatory Note cross-reference to heading 2209 part (I) second paragraph (1) is an administrative reference that is not actionable in OWL without the underlying text. No regulatory individuals (MeasureType, Footnote) for this subheading appear in the running TBox, so none are referenced.
